_readme: External example — 400 Bad Request when wrong type is sent (integer for a string field). The schema defines 'designation' as type: string. Sending an integer (99) should return 400, not silently coerce or crash. This is a schema resiliency scenario surfaced as an external example — useful when the type enforcement is handled by the server, not the framework.

body.message: Field 'designation' must be a string, received integer
